top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Shielded Token Operations: Mint, Transfer & Burn with Test Suite
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Walk through the complete shielded token lifecycle in Compact: minting, transferring, and burning, with compilable code and a full test suite. No UI required, this is focused on the contract and testing layer. Covers: - Minting with `mintShieldedToken` and `evolveNonce` - Transferring with `sendShielded` and change management via `ShieldedSendResult` - Burning via `sendImmediateShielded` to `shieldedBurnAddress()` - The Merkle tree constraint (freshly m

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Replay Attack Prevention in Compact: Nonces, Nullifiers & Domain Separation
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Explain three replay prevention mechanisms: (1) Counter-based nonces that increment per operation, (2) Set-based nullifiers derived from `persistentCommit(secret, context)`, (3) Domain separation tags in hash functions to prevent cross-circuit replay. Show working examples of each and when to use which pattern. ## Target Audience Developers ## Deliverables 1. **Written tutorial (2,500-3,500 words)** covering: - Counter-based nonces that increment per op

top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Concurrent Transactions on Midnight: UTXO Race Conditions & Workarounds
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Explain why two transactions from the same wallet can fail if they try to spend the same DUST UTXOs. top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] Midnight vs Other Privacy Chains: Architecture Comparison for Developers
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Compare Midnight's architecture (Compact + ZK circuits + Zswap + dual ledger) with other privacy-focused chains (Aztec's Noir + UTXO, Aleo's Leo + records, Mina's o1js + recursive SNARKs, Zcash's Sapling). Focus on developer experience: language design, state model, privacy model. top_opportunities.title: [Tutorial] DUST Sponsorship: How One Wallet Pays Fees for Another User's Transaction
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Type of Content **Tutorial** ## Summary Explain the sponsorship flow where a backend wallet calls `balanceUnboundTransaction` with `tokenKindsToBalance: ["dust"]` on behalf of a user who has no DUST. top_opportunities.title: Make it easier to write reliable cleanup tasks
top_opportunities.body_preview: [](https://issuehunt.io/r/avajs/ava/issues/928) See: https://github.com/avajs/ava/pull/918#issuecomment-227016238 `after.always` has some issues when used as a cleanup task. Specifically, it won't run if: - There are test failures and `--fail-fast` is used. - There are uncaught exceptions thrown. I've advocated using `.before` or `.beforeEach` to ensure s
